story.iWant: to see visual progress bars when converting multiple preset files
story.soThat: I can monitor batch conversion status and estimated completion time

task.title: Implement Conversion Trigger (AC-1)
task.subtask: Add 'c' key handler to Model.Update() with selection check
Create format selection prompt component with Lipgloss styled menu
Create output directory prompt with validation
Implement confirmation screen showing file count, formats, output dir
Add unit tests for trigger flow

task.title: Overall Progress Bar (AC-2)
task.subtask: Add progress tracking fields to Model (converting, currentFile, totalFiles, completedFiles, startTime)
Create renderProgressBar(current, total int) string function with percentage and bar
Implement progress bar color logic (blue in-progress, green completed, red errors)
Update progress bar in real-time using Bubbletea Cmd tick
Add unit tests for progress calculation and rendering

task.title: Per-File Status Display (AC-3)
task.subtask: Add per-file tracking fields (currentFileName, currentStatus, completedList, remainingCount)
Create formatFileStatus(file, sourceFormat, targetFormat, status) function with color-coding
Update status during conversion (converting → success/warning/error)
Render completed files list (last 5 files)
Add unit tests for status formatting

task.title: Time Estimation (AC-4)
task.subtask: Add time tracking fields (startTime, elapsedTime, estimatedRemaining)
Create formatDuration(d time.Duration) string function (MM:SS format)
Implement estimateRemainingTime(start, completed, total) calculation
Update time every second using Bubbletea tick Cmd
Add unit tests for time formatting and estimation

task.title: Results Summary (AC-5)
task.subtask: Add ConversionResult struct and results tracking
Accumulate results during conversion with success/warning/error counts
Create renderSummaryScreen(results) function with color-coded statistics
Show summary after batch completes with any-key dismiss
Add unit tests for summary generation

task.title: Cancellation Handling (AC-6)
task.subtask: Add cancellation fields (cancelling bool, cancelChan chan bool)
Implement Ctrl+C handler setting cancelling state
Check cancel signal in conversion loop before each file
Display partial results on cancel with cancelled count
Add unit tests for cancellation logic

task.title: Error Handling (AC-7)
task.subtask: Implement per-file error catching with recover from panic
Track errors during batch without halting
Log error details using slog
Display errors in summary with red color-coding
Add unit tests for error recovery

task.title: Integration and Polish
task.subtask: Integrate with converter.Convert() from Epic 1
Add spinner animation for current file
Optimize with parallel conversion using worker pool pattern
End-to-end testing with 10+ sample files
Update help overlay with 'c' key and Ctrl+C documentation

criterion.title: Batch Conversion Trigger
criterion.requirement: 'c' key triggers conversion when files are selected (from Story 4-1 multi-selection)
Target format selection prompt appears before conversion starts
User selects target format: NP3, XMP, or lrtemplate via numbered menu
Output directory selection prompt (default: same directory as source)
Confirmation screen shows: file count, source formats, target format, output location
User confirms (y) or cancels (n/Esc) before conversion begins

criterion.title: Overall Progress Bar
criterion.requirement: Display progress bar showing overall batch completion (X/Y files)
Progress bar fills left-to-right as conversions complete
Percentage indicator shows numeric progress (e.g., "50% (5/10)")
Bar color: blue (in-progress), green (completed), red (errors detected)
Progress bar updates in real-time as each file completes
Bar remains visible for 3 seconds after batch completes

criterion.title: Per-File Conversion Status
criterion.requirement: Display current file being processed (filename shown)
Show conversion status: "Converting...", "✓ Success", "⚠️ Warning", "✗ Error"
Color-code status: Blue (converting), Green (success), Yellow (warning), Red (error)
Display file number in sequence (e.g., "File 3 of 10")
Show source format → target format for current file

criterion.title: Time Estimation
criterion.requirement: Display elapsed time since batch started (MM:SS format)
Estimate remaining time based on average file conversion time
Show "Remaining: ~MM:SS" with tilde indicating estimate
Update estimate every second as batch progresses
Handle edge cases: first file (no estimate yet), very fast conversions (<1s)

criterion.title: Color-Coded Results Summary
criterion.requirement: After batch completes, show summary screen with statistics
Display total files: succeeded (green), warnings (yellow), errors (red)
List all warning files with warning messages
List all error files with error messages
Show total elapsed time for entire batch
Press any key to return to file browser

criterion.title: Cancellable Operations
criterion.requirement: Ctrl+C immediately cancels batch conversion
Show "Cancelling..." message while in-flight conversion completes
Display partial results summary (files completed before cancel)
No partial/corrupted output files left (atomic writes or cleanup)
Return to file browser with selection preserved

criterion.title: Error Handling and Recovery
criterion.requirement: Detect and report conversion errors per file (don't halt batch)
Continue to next file if current file fails
Log error details for debugging
Show error count in progress bar (e.g., "8/10, 2 errors")
Option to retry failed files after batch completes

doc.title: Product Requirements Document
doc.snippet: Requirement: Visual progress bars for batch operations showing overall progress (X/Y files), current file processing, estimated time remaining. Real-time updates, color-coded (success/warning/error), cancellable (Ctrl+C).

doc.title: Product Requirements Document
doc.snippet: TUI provides interactive file browsing with live parameter preview, batch progress visualization, and visual validation before conversion. Built with Bubbletea framework for keyboard-driven workflows.

doc.title: Architecture Document
doc.snippet: Bubbletea Elm Architecture (Model-Update-View) with Lipgloss styling. Real-time updates via Cmd pattern for progress animation. State immutability with pure functions.

doc.title: Architecture Document
doc.snippet: Target: Convert 100 files in <10 seconds. Use worker pool pattern with runtime.NumCPU() goroutines. Update progress atomically with mutex on shared state.

doc.title: Story 4.1 - Bubbletea File Browser
doc.snippet: Story 4-1 provides file selection mechanism (selected map[int]bool). Story 4-3 uses selected files as input for batch conversion triggered by 'c' key.

doc.title: Story 4.2 - Live Parameter Preview
doc.snippet: Story 4-2 implements detectUnmappableParams() for identifying parameters that won't map between formats. Story 4-3 reuses this to show warnings during batch conversion.

doc.title: Story 1.9 - Bidirectional Conversion API
doc.snippet: converter.Convert(input []byte, sourceFormat string, targetFormat string) ([]byte, error) - Single conversion API shared across all interfaces. Returns ConversionError for type-safe error handling.

artifact.reason: Core conversion API called for each file in batch. Returns converted data or ConversionError. Implemented in Story 1-9.
Type-safe error handling for conversion failures. Used to distinguish conversion errors from other failures during batch processing.
NP3 parser used for source format detection and validation before batch conversion.
XMP parser used for source format detection and validation before batch conversion.
lrtemplate parser used for source format detection and validation before batch conversion.

go-modules.module: Core TUI framework with Cmd pattern for async updates
Terminal styling for progress bars and colors

technical.constraint: BLOCKS ON: Story 4-1 (Bubbletea File Browser) - Must be implemented first to provide file selection mechanism
COORDINATES WITH: Story 4-2 (Live Parameter Preview) - Reuses detectUnmappableParams() for warning detection
REQUIRES: Story 1-9 (Bidirectional Conversion API) - converter.Convert() must be functional
Must use Bubbletea v2 experimental (/v2 import paths) with Cmd pattern for async updates
Performance target: Convert 100 files in <10 seconds (avg 100ms per file)
Worker pool concurrency: runtime.NumCPU() goroutines for parallel conversion
Atomic file writes: Use temp file + rename pattern to prevent corrupted output
Must work on Windows, macOS, Linux terminals with minimum 80x24 size

architectural.constraint: Extend Stories 4-1 and 4-2 Model - Add batch conversion state (converting, currentFile, results, startTime)
New conversion screen replaces file browser during batch (switch based on converting flag)
Progress updates via Bubbletea tick Cmd (100ms interval for 10 FPS animation)
Worker pool pattern: Job queue (chan FileInfo), result queue (chan ConversionResult), WaitGroup for synchronization
Thread safety: Mutex on shared state (currentFile counter) during parallel conversion
State immutability: Follow Bubbletea Elm Architecture - pure functions for Update/View
Return to file browser on completion/cancellation with selection preserved

ui-ux.constraint: Progress bar: 40 characters width, filled blocks (█) and empty blocks (░)
Color coding: Blue (in-progress), Green (completed), Yellow (warnings), Red (errors)
Time format: MM:SS for <1 hour batches, HH:MM:SS for longer batches
Confirmation screen shows: file count, source formats breakdown, target format, output directory
Summary screen: Color-coded statistics, warning/error details, total elapsed time

performance.constraint: Parallel conversion efficiency: 8-core CPU should complete 100 files in ~3 seconds (8 parallel × 25ms avg)
Progress bar update frequency: 100ms (10 FPS) for smooth animation without CPU overhead
Time estimation accuracy: Within ±20% of actual completion time after first 3 files
Cancellation responsiveness: <200ms from Ctrl+C to cancelling message display

error-handling.constraint: Per-file error isolation: Conversion errors don't halt batch processing
Panic recovery: Use defer/recover in worker goroutines to prevent crash
Error logging: Use slog.Error() with filename, source/target formats, and error message
Partial file cleanup: Remove temp files on error or cancellation
Error count visibility: Show error count in progress bar (e.g., "8/10, 2 errors")

interface.name: convertBatch
interface.description: Main batch conversion orchestrator using worker pool pattern. Returns results for all files processed.

interface.name: renderProgressBar
interface.signature: renderProgressBar(current int, total int, errorCount int) string
interface.description: Render progress bar with filled/empty blocks, percentage, and error count if > 0.

interface.name: formatFileStatus
interface.signature: formatFileStatus(file string, sourceFormat string, targetFormat string, status string) string
interface.description: Format per-file status line with icon, color coding, and format conversion display.

interface.name: estimateRemainingTime
interface.signature: estimateRemainingTime(start time.Time, completed int, total int) time.Duration
interface.description: Calculate remaining time estimate based on average conversion time per file.

interface.name: formatDuration
interface.description: Format duration as MM:SS or HH:MM:SS string.

interface.name: renderSummaryScreen
interface.description: Render final summary with color-coded statistics, warning/error details, and total time.

interface.name: writeOutputAtomic
interface.description: Write file atomically using temp file + rename to prevent partial writes.

fields.field: Message string - Error/warning message if applicable
converting bool - Batch conversion in progress
totalFiles int - Total files in batch
cancelChan chan bool - Signal to stop batch

interface.name: Keyboard events
interface.keys: c: Trigger batch conversion (when files selected)
1-3: Select target format in prompt menu

interface.name: converter.Convert
interface.signature: Convert(input []byte, sourceFormat string, targetFormat string) ([]byte, error)
interface.description: Core conversion API from Story 1-9. Called for each file in batch. Returns ConversionError on failure.

tests.standards:
Story 4-3 follows the same testing standards as Stories 4-1 and 4-2:
- Unit tests for batch logic using Go's testing package with table-driven test pattern - Test coverage target: 85% minimum for cmd/tui/ package - Integration tests with real sample files (10+ file batches) - Performance tests: 100 files in <10 seconds target - Manual cross-platform testing on Windows (PowerShell), macOS (Terminal.app), Linux (gnome-terminal) - Concurrency testing: Race detector enabled during worker pool tests - Visual inspection: Progress bar animation, color coding, spinner
Testing framework: Go standard library testing package Concurrency testing: go test -race to detect race conditions Performance benchmarking: go test -bench for conversion throughput CI/CD: Tests run in GitHub Actions on all platforms

locations.location: cmd/tui/batch_test.go - Unit tests for batch conversion logic and worker pool
cmd/tui/progress_test.go - Unit tests for progress bar rendering and time estimation
cmd/tui/model_test.go - Extended tests for Model.Update() with 'c' key and conversion state
examples/np3/ - 22 NP3 sample files for batch conversion testing
examples/xmp/ - 913 XMP sample files for batch conversion testing
examples/lrtemplate/ - 544 lrtemplate sample files for batch conversion testing

ideas.test-idea: TestConversionTrigger - Verify 'c' key disabled when no selection; test format menu navigation; test confirmation screen
TestFormatSelection - Test numbered menu (1-3), Esc to cancel, invalid input handling
TestProgressBarRendering - Verify bar at 0%, 50%, 100%; test width calculation; test color logic (blue/green/red)
TestProgressPercentage - Test percentage accuracy for various current/total combinations
TestFileStatusFormatting - Test status icons (✓⚠️✗), color coding, format display (NP3→XMP)
TestCompletedList - Test last 5 files display, scrolling for longer lists
TestTimeEstimation - Test elapsed time accuracy; test remaining time calculation; test edge cases (0 completed, first file)
TestDurationFormatting - Test MM:SS format, HH:MM:SS for long batches, "0:00" for zero
TestResultsSummary - Test count accuracy (success/warning/error); test message formatting; test color coding
TestSummaryDismissal - Test any-key returns to file browser
TestCancellation - Test Ctrl+C stops batch; test in-flight file completes; test partial results display
TestSelectionPreserved - Verify file selection preserved after cancellation
TestErrorRecovery - Test batch continues after error; test error count tracking; test error message capture
TestErrorIsolation - Verify errors don't halt batch processing
TestBatchConversion10Files - Convert 10 mixed format files; verify all complete; verify results accurate
TestBatchConversionErrors - Include corrupted file in batch; verify error handling; verify batch completes
TestBatchConversion100Files - Convert 100 files; measure total time (<10s target); verify throughput
TestWorkerPoolScaling - Test conversion with 1, 2, 4, 8 workers; verify parallel speedup
TestRaceConditions - Run with -race flag; test worker pool; verify no data races on shared state
TestAtomicWrites - Test multiple workers writing simultaneously; verify no corrupted files
